name: Sofitel Kia Ora Moorea Beach Resort

content.verdict: Sofitel Kia Ora is Moorea's gentle answer to Bora Bora — same turquoise lagoon, same overwater bungalows, a fraction of the price, and a lush mountain backdrop (Cook's Bay and Mount Rotui) that is arguably more beautiful than Otemanu. The resort sits on Temae Beach, the best beach on Moorea, a long soft-sand strip facing Tahiti across the lagoon. The property has 60 bungalows mixing beachfront and overwater, a lovely infinity pool, and a proper Sofitel sense of French-Polynesian polish without the stiffness. Overwater bungalows are updated with glass floor panels, outdoor showers, and ladders straight into the lagoon. Service is warm, the food is surprisingly good (the French breakfast alone is a reason to stay), and honeymooners love the sunrise-over-Tahiti views east-facing from the overwater pontoon. The smartest honeymoon playbook in French Polynesia: 4 nights here on Moorea, 3 nights overwater on Bora Bora. You get two islands, two moods, and more variety than staying in one place the whole time. Moorea is also easier and cheaper to reach — 15-minute flight or 45-minute ferry from Papeete.
content.best_room: The Luxury Overwater Bungalow End-of-Pontoon is the book — deepest water, best views of Tahiti at sunrise and Moorea's mountains at sunset. The Beach Bungalow with Plunge Pool is a strong alternative for couples who prefer beachfront over overwater. Skip garden categories.
caveats: Moorea's lagoon is calmer and less dramatically blue than Bora Bora's — still beautiful, but a different water color (greener, more turquoise).
Some overwater bungalows are older than the most recent refresh; specify renovated room at booking.
Food and drink pricing is high (half-board plan helps).
Resort is not on a private motu — the road passes near the beach side, though the overwater pontoon is fully private.
Kids welcome but the resort is couples-dominant.

faqs.q: Moorea vs. Bora Bora for a honeymoon?
faqs.a: Bora Bora has the bluer lagoon and the iconic Otemanu photo. Moorea has more dramatic green mountains, more activity options on land (hiking, 4x4, ATV), and is half the price. Best answer: do both, starting Moorea and ending Bora Bora.

faqs.q: Is Sofitel Kia Ora the best Moorea resort?
faqs.a: It is one of the top 2-3 alongside Hilton Moorea and Intercontinental Moorea. Sofitel wins on beach quality (Temae) and sunrise views. Hilton wins on lagoon water clarity. Pick based on view priorities.

faqs.q: Can we use Accor Live Limitless points?
faqs.a: Yes. Sofitel Kia Ora is a standard ALL redemption and one of the most aspirational. Book 9-12 months out for award space.
Temae Beach is the best on Moorea — long, soft white sand, shallow for a long walk out, and facing Tahiti for dramatic sunrises.

faqs.q: How do we get to Moorea?
faqs.a: 15-minute flight from Papeete, or 45-minute Aremiti/Terevau ferry — both work well. The ferry is scenic and significantly cheaper.

faqs.q: Best time to combine Moorea and Bora Bora?
faqs.a: May-October. If possible August-September for humpback whale season off Moorea (you can swim with them via licensed operators).

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking — Sofitel Kia Ora Moorea, [dates]

Hi,

We are planning a honeymoon and would like to book a Luxury Overwater Bungalow End-of-Pontoon for [X] nights arriving [date].

Please confirm:
- Availability and best honeymoon rate
- Confirmation the bungalow is a refreshed category
- Accor Live Limitless points option if available
- Honeymoon amenity inclusions (champagne, in-bungalow breakfast, turndown)
- Half-board dinner plan pricing
- Couples spa treatment availability
- A private sunset cocktail setup on the overwater deck
- Lagoon tour and whale-watching operator recommendations

Thank you,
[Names]
